name: github-pixel-squad-flow
displayName: Pixel Crew — Your Dev Teammates (Pixel Squad Flow)
description: Your one-person-army dev crew in VS Code. Talk to frontend, backend, tester, devops and designer teammates from GitHub Copilot Chat — they plan, code, run commands, and keep a shared work log.

commands.title: Pixel Squad Flow: Show Agent Factory
Pixel Squad Flow: Route Task From Command Palette
Pixel Squad Flow: Open in Editor Panel
Pixel Squad Flow: Assign Task to Agent
Pixel Squad Flow: Reset Workspace State
Pixel Squad Flow: Run Smoke Test
Pixel Squad Flow: Toggle Force MCP-First

pixelSquad.autoExecute.description: Automatically execute tasks and auto-apply produced file edits to the workspace. Keep this on for the one-person-army experience. Disable only if you want a manual review step before every change.

pixelSquad.forceMcpForAllTasks.description: Force MCP-first tool selection for every task, even when the task looks like a local workspace edit. Leave this off unless you want external tools prioritized across the entire squad.

enumDescriptions: Use GitHub Copilot language models for planning and execution.
Use Claude language models (requires Anthropic extension or Claude Code).
pixelSquad.modelFamily.description: Preferred language model family for planning and task execution.

pixelSquad.autoPopulateWorkspaceContext.description: Automatically capture active editor content, open files, git changes, and relevant workspace files when creating or assigning a task.

pixelSquad.workspaceContextMaxFiles.description: Maximum number of workspace files to auto-include as task context during planning and execution.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Pixel Squad Flow
walkthroughs.description: Learn how to route tasks through your chat-first multi-agent factory powered by GitHub Copilot.

steps.title: Open the Agent Factory
steps.description:
Open the Pixel Squad Flow panel to see your rooms, agents, and task wall.

[Open Agent Factory](command:pixelSquad.showFactory)

steps.title: Start From Copilot Chat
steps.description: Use `@pixel-squad` in GitHub Copilot Chat to start a run. Add persona commands like `/frontend` or `/tester` to engage a specific lane. The runtime panel updates automatically.

steps.title: Chat with @pixel-squad
steps.description: Type `@pixel-squad` in GitHub Copilot Chat to route tasks conversationally. The squad updates the factory panel in real time.

steps.title: Execute and Review Tasks
steps.description: Click Execute on any queued task to have an agent work on it using GitHub Copilot. Review the output and approve or retry.

steps.title: Run the Smoke Test
steps.description:
Verify everything works end-to-end with a single command.

[Run Smoke Test](command:pixelSquad.runSmokeTest)

chatParticipants.name: pixel-squad
chatParticipants.description: Route a task through Pixel Squad Flow (Copilot + Claude) and update the Agent Factory.

commands.name: route
commands.description: Route a task through the squad and update the factory.

commands.name: room
commands.description: Create a room for a theme. Usage: /room <theme> [name].

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show the current squad status: rooms, agents, and active lanes.

commands.name: mcp
commands.description: List MCP and extension tools available to Pixel Squad agents.

commands.name: ask
commands.description: Talk to a specific teammate. Usage: /ask <agent or persona> <question>.

commands.name: team
commands.description: Rally a whole team on one task. Usage: /team <theme> <task>.

commands.name: worklog
commands.description: Show a rollup of what teammates have been working on. Usage: /worklog [agent or team].

commands.name: lead
commands.description: Send this request directly to the lead persona.

commands.name: frontend
commands.description: Send this request directly to the frontend persona.

commands.name: backend
commands.description: Send this request directly to the backend persona.

commands.name: tester
commands.description: Send this request directly to the tester persona.

commands.name: devops
commands.description: Send this request directly to the devops persona.

commands.name: designer
commands.description: Send this request directly to the designer persona.

scripts.build: npm run build:webview && npm run build:extension
scripts.package:vsix: npm run build && npx @vscode/vsce package --out github-pixel-squad-flow.vsix
